questions: what is the difference between RAM and ROM?
ram ar rom er difference ki?
ssd ar hdd er difference ki?
what are the main parts of a computer?
answers: RAM (Random Access Memory) is your computer's short-term memory. It temporarily stores data that the CPU needs to access quickly. When you turn off the computer, RAM data is erased.
ROM (Read-Only Memory) is permanent storage that holds essential instructions for booting up the computer. Unlike RAM, ROM retains its data even when the power is off.
CPU (Central Processing Unit) is the brain of the computer. It processes instructions from programs, performs calculations, and manages data flow between all hardware components.

GPU (Graphics Processing Unit) is specialized for rendering images, videos, and animations. While a CPU handles general tasks, a GPU excels at parallel processing, making it essential for gaming and AI workloads.

SSD (Solid State Drive) uses flash memory with no moving parts, making it much faster and more durable than HDD. HDD (Hard Disk Drive) uses spinning magnetic disks and is cheaper per GB but significantly slower.
The main parts of a computer include the CPU, RAM, storage (SSD/HDD), motherboard, power supply (PSU), and GPU. The motherboard connects all these components together and allows them to communicate.
A motherboard is the main circuit board of a computer. It houses the CPU, RAM slots, storage connectors, and expansion slots, acting as the central hub that connects all components together.
Computer er main parts holo: CPU (processor), RAM (memory), SSD/HDD (storage), motherboard, power supply, ar GPU (graphics card). Shob miliye computer kaj kore.
